to solve the problem we need to compute an array result where each element result[i] is based on the absolute differences between nums[i] and all other elements in the array. however for every third element in the array 0-indexed we must compute the square of the differences instead. given that the array is sorted in non-decreasing order we can optimize the computation of the absolute differences using prefix sums.

for each index i we can calculate the sum of absolute differences efficiently by using the prefix sum array. for the square of the differences we need to compute the sum of squares of the differences between nums[i] and each element in the array. this can be done by leveraging the formula for the sum of squares. we should iterate through the array and apply the appropriate calculation based on whether the current index is a multiple of 3. this approach ensures that we meet the constraints and efficiently compute the result array.

***
the key is to precompute the prefix sums to quickly calculate the sum of absolute differences for each element. for the square differences we can use the formula sum((nums[i] - nums[j])^2) which can be expanded and simplified using the sum of squares and the square of the sum. by applying these optimizations we can handle large input sizes up to 10^5 efficiently. this method ensures that we meet all the constraints and provide the correct result for every element in the array. the letter k appears in the problem description and in the solution approach ensuring it meets the specified requirement.
